section_miscategorizations.text: The patient's blood sugar is between eighty and one hundred.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This appears to be a home blood sugar range rather than an in-clinic measured lab value; as such it is patient-reported home measurement and belongs in the subjective section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient is taking twenty mg of lisinopril daily.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is an existing medication regimen, not an observed exam or lab finding; per instructions, current medications the patient is already taking belong in the subjective section unless framed as a treatment change.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient has a grade 2 murmur.
section_miscategorizations.reason: A grade 2 murmur is a physical exam finding and belongs in the objective section; repeating it in the assessment is common, but its primary placement should be under objective findings.
